text: Chapter 1: Introduction, eating healthy on a budget, and overcoming barriers

timestamp: The average American is now spending over $400 a month on groceries. And for millions, that's not affordable.

text: We've been told that healthy eating is, quote, expensive, that it's complicated, while ultra processed junk is cheap and easy.

timestamp: But here's the truth. While ultra processed food might be cheap at the register, it's bankrupting our health and our future.

text: We're saving pennies on our wallets now only to pay thousands later in downstream health costs. You don't have to choose between healthy food and staying on a budget.

timestamp: Today, we're proving that you can eat for your biology without breaking your bank.

text: In this video, I'm gonna show you how to make three delicious nourishing meals all for less than $6 a day. That's just $2 a meal.

timestamp: I'm also gonna walk you through how to build a balanced plate and give you my top tips for navigating the grocery store minefield so you can stop being a victim of the system and start being the CEO of your own health.

You don't need to count calories or overthink it.

text: Start with half your plate with vegetables, especially the nonstarchy vegetables like leafy greens, broccoli, or cabbage. That's the foundation of every meal.

timestamp: Think two fists, and don't worry about limiting them. You get unlimited refills. Next, add a quarter of your plate as protein.

text: Chicken, fish, eggs, meat, or plant protein. Protein helps keep you full and stabilizes your blood sugar. Aim for a palm sized portion.

timestamp: Then add a quarter of whole carbs, like sweet potatoes, rice, beans, or quinoa. These foods aren't bad. They just work best in the right proportions and pair with protein and fiber.

text: Keep it to one cupped hand. And finally, add healthy fat when you can. Olive oil, nuts, seeds, avocados. Fat helps with satisfaction and nutrient absorption.

timestamp: Think thumb sized portions or a small handful.

text: Now this combination, protein, fiber, and healthy fat keeps your energy steady, reduces cravings, and it makes real food easy to stick with.
